name: wormhole
description: Evidence-gated planning and orchestration state for AI coding agents.
long_description: Wormhole exposes a local MCP server for evidence records, open-question gates, native context packs, context-pack budget review, reversible optimization, optimized command execution, local orchestration runs, orchestration task state, graph-first repo indexing, durable graph communities, surprising connection ranking, graph wiki pages, graph-node semantic search, named execution flow artifacts, repo watch sessions, git diff detection, repo activity recording, state maintenance coordination, project contract detection, structured diagnostics, impact-aware verification planning, safety scanning, deterministic semantic fallback search, safe LSP probes, LSP feedback replanning, one-shot project onboarding, repo blueprint and constraints artifacts, app-process product/roadmap/backlog artifacts, native architecture maps, entrypoint flow discovery, blast-radius analysis, task-scoped project context packs, agent-facing routing, layered tool maps, structured tool catalog queries, next-tool recommendations, mission routes, prepared agent context, SQLite-backed durable repo indexes, durable semantic indexes, process-local LSP sessions, dependency security and live dependency risk reports, git lifecycle branch/commit/PR preparation, bounded conflict analysis, docs-sync gates, workspace graph analysis, action admission policy, patch transactions, optimization adapters, required Python runtime, native media ingestion, shell hook management, discovery-driven tool generation, learned orchestration policy gates, safe live policy feedback, policy baseline comparison, reasoning strategy research, printed-tool execution, native tool generation, deterministic conductor scaffolds, durable behavior policy, external agent dispatch, shared agent workspace memory, durable resume continuation, model-profile traces, typed artifacts, and workbench rendering.

tools.name: mission_start
tools.description: Start an evidence-gated coding, review, onboarding, or planning mission.

tools.name: round_start
tools.description: Start a gather and reason round.

tools.name: record_evidence
tools.description: Record sourced mission evidence.

tools.name: record_question
tools.description: Record an open question or assumption gap.

tools.name: gate_request
tools.description: Evaluate whether the mission can produce its final response or requested artifact.

tools.name: emit_plan
tools.description: Emit an evidence-cited Markdown plan only for explicit plan, spec, or design requests.

tools.name: ctx_record
tools.description: Record native source-backed context.

tools.name: ctx_pack_query
tools.description: Query native context records.

tools.name: ctx_pack_create
tools.description: Create a budgeted context pack.

tools.name: ctx_pack_budget_review
tools.description: Review explicit context-pack retention and eviction decisions.

tools.name: ctx_pack_refresh
tools.description: Refresh a context pack with budget and stale-record eviction rules.

tools.name: ctx_pack_render
tools.description: Render a context pack.

tools.name: optimization_apply
tools.description: Apply reversible native optimization.

tools.name: optimization_retrieve
tools.description: Retrieve original optimized content.

tools.name: optimized_command_run
tools.description: Run a command with reversible output optimization.

tools.name: optimization_stats
tools.description: Read aggregate optimization savings stats.

tools.name: task_register
tools.description: Register a tracked orchestration task.

tools.name: orchestration_plan_local
tools.description: Plan a local adapter-free orchestration run.

tools.name: orchestration_run_local
tools.description: Execute a local deterministic orchestration run from supplied outcomes.

tools.name: agent_register
tools.description: Register an external AI agent or provider worker.

tools.name: agent_dispatch
tools.description: Dispatch a task to a registered external agent.

tools.name: agent_dispatch_execute

tools.name: agent_status
tools.description: Read external agent run status.

tools.name: agent_interrupt
tools.description: Interrupt an external agent run when supported.

tools.name: agent_complete
tools.description: Record external agent run completion.

tools.name: agent_workspace_create
tools.description: Create shared mission workspace memory for concurrent agents.

tools.description: Write an attributed agent workspace record.

tools.name: agent_workspace_read
tools.description: Read shared agent workspace records.

tools.name: agent_workspace_merge

prompts.name: wormhole_orchestrate
prompts.description: Run a structured Wormhole coding, review, onboarding, planning, or orchestration mission.
prompts.text:
Use Wormhole for this mission: ${arguments.objective}

Follow the Wormhole loop strictly: mission_start, round_start, gather evidence, record_evidence, record_question for gaps, register tasks when parallel work is useful, use ctx_record and ctx_pack_create for native context packs, use ctx_pack_budget_review and ctx_pack_refresh when context packs overflow or stale records need eviction, use optimization_apply or optimized_command_run when large outputs need reversible compaction, use orchestration_plan_local for local task waves and orchestration_run_local for deterministic local outcomes, and gate_request before final claims. For coding tasks, continue into implementation and verification; only call emit_plan when the user explicitly asks for a plan, spec, design, or planning-only artifact. Start from workflow_start_feature, workflow_fix_bug, workflow_review_pr, or workflow_onboard_repo when the task matches those golden paths, and use workflow_write_artifacts when durable workflow run state is needed; use resume_record for material decisions, blockers, verification, handoff, final response, and fresh-session recommendations; use resume_checkpoint before final output, heavy-context handoff, or a new chat, and resume_validate before claiming a session is safely resumable; use project_intelligence_snapshot, tool_layer_map, tool_exposure_profile, tool_catalog_query, tool_admission_review, next_best_tool, mission_route, agent_context_prepare, state_maintenance_run, state_maintenance_status, and state_maintenance_retry to choose a curated path, select a visible profile, review risky tools, and keep graph/context/evidence/workspace state synchronized before selecting lower-level tools; use project_onboard first when entering a new or changed project, then blueprint_compile_repo or blueprint_write_artifacts to produce repo-specific agent rules and blueprint_gate_check before completion claims; use architecture_map, entrypoint_flow_discover, blast_radius_analyze, and context_pack_generate when native large-repo intelligence is useful; use durable_repo_index_refresh, durable_index_manifest_refresh, durable_index_manifest_status, durable_repo_index_query, repo_graph_refresh_full, durable_semantic_index_refresh, test_impact_analyze_v2, dependency_security_report, dependency_risk_report, dependency_audit_live, git_lifecycle_status, git_branch_prepare, git_commit_prepare, git_pr_prepare, git_conflict_analyze, docs_sync_check, workspace_graph_analyze, action_policy_review, patch_checkpoint, patch_apply, patch_status, patch_rollback, lsp_session_start, lsp_session_request, lsp_feedback_replan, and optimization_adapter_run when deeper project intelligence is useful. Use git_branch_create and git_commit_create only after tool_admission_review/action_policy_review because they mutate git state. Use project_contract_detect, dependency_inventory, project_command_map, lsp_probe, diagnostics_from_command, diagnostics_query, impact_analyze, test_plan_select, verification_run, secret_scan, operation_risk_review, semantic_index_build, and semantic_search for lower-level project ground truth. Use repo_index_build and repo_index_query before broad grep or file reads for codebase questions; use repo_index_explain, repo_index_path, repo_index_report, repo_graph_analyze, repo_graph_export, python_graph_metrics, python_graph_communities, graph_communities_refresh, list_communities, get_community, get_surprising_connections, graph_node_semantic_index_refresh, graph_node_semantic_search, flows_refresh, list_flows, get_flow, and graph_wiki_generate when relationships, communities, graph-node search, or execution paths matter. Use media_dependency_report, media_ingest_pdf, and media_ingest_image for repo-local PDFs/images. Use shell_hook_plan before shell hook edits and require explicit apply for shell_hook_install or shell_hook_uninstall. Use discovery_har_import, discovery_openapi_import, discovery_http_crawl, discovery_browser_capture, and discovery_tool_spec_generate before generating API tools; browser capture is complementary input, not the core runtime. Use printing_press_register, printing_press_verify, printing_press_run, printing_press_register_agent, tool_factory_generate, tool_factory_validate, and tool_factory_write for generated tools when their capabilities match the task. Use agent_workspace_create, agent_workspace_write, agent_workspace_read, and agent_workspace_merge when concurrent workers need shared state. Use agent_dispatch_execute only for agents with configured CLI or HTTP runtimes. Use model_profile_select, model_profile_record_outcome, conductor_plan, conductor_replay, orchestration_trace_record, orchestration_dataset_export, orchestration_policy_train, orchestration_policy_evaluate, orchestration_policy_compare_baselines, orchestration_policy_activate, and orchestration_policy_live_feedback when comparing local model profiles or replay-gating orchestration policy. Use reasoning_trace_record, reasoning_dataset_export, and reasoning_strategy_evaluate when plan, critique, revision, and verifier quality should feed research. Use behavior_mode_set and behavior_minimality_review when dense output or minimality review is useful.
